story_purpose: Map the path from sparse counts to embeddings, recurrent sequence state, fixed-vector seq2seq bottleneck, attention relief, and the Transformer handoff.
caption_contract: Figure 2.x - The Bottleneck Before The Breakthrough. The diagram shows technical pressures that made attention and Transformer blocks useful; it is not a complete NLP history or priority claim.
blocked_claims: First-invention claims; state-of-the-art rankings; hidden industrial adoption; attention-as-consciousness; complete NLP history.
next_gate: Build as one rights-safe SVG after confirming Chapter 1/2 final numbering.

story_purpose: Show token embeddings, position signal, query/key/value projections, attention weights, value mixing, multi-head branch, and feed-forward/residual wrapper.
caption_contract: Figure 3.x - Attention Is A Mixing Operation, Not A Mind. Self-attention routes information among token positions, but it does not prove intention, truth, grounding, faithful explanation, or modern LLM implementation details.
blocked_claims: Human-like attention; consciousness; full modern decoder-only implementation; distance-is-free; stable head semantics; solved truth/grounding.
next_gate: Build before any later architecture schematic so readers have a substrate visual.

story_purpose: Contrast recurrent stepwise state passing with self-attention routes across positions and a parallel-training opportunity lane.
caption_contract: Figure 3.x - The Break In The Loop. Self-attention changes the route of information flow and training parallelism; it does not eliminate all context cost or explain later product dominance by itself.
blocked_claims: Parallelism-alone causality; context cost disappearance; long-context solved; hardware-alone selection; eternal Transformer dominance.
next_gate: Use only if the self-attention schematic needs a companion; otherwise fold into CH2-4VIS-002.

story_purpose: Show measured loss, modeled extrapolation, industry interpretation, bounded GPT-3/PaLM examples, and blocked capability/truth/safety/product claims as separate lanes.
caption_contract: Figure 4.x - Loss Is A Map, Not A Product Guarantee. Scaling evidence can support measured loss and compute/data/model tradeoffs, but not automatic truth, safety, product success, or emergence thresholds.
blocked_claims: Exact exponents; plotted Kaplan values; universal token/parameter formula; emergence cliffs; benchmark values; revenue; product success; safety from scale.
next_gate: Build as schematic unless exact values are extracted and row-audited.

story_purpose: Show compute allocation among model size, training tokens, and data/optimization quality, with Chinchilla as a balance warning.
caption_contract: Figure 4.x - Scale Became Allocation. Compute must be spent across model size, data, and training quality; bigger parameter count alone is not the sourced claim.
blocked_claims: Bigger-is-always-better; exact Chinchilla formula; universal token ratio; dataset-specific claims; compute as business proof.
next_gate: Make a second Chapter 4 figure only if the evidence-lane chart is too dense.

story_purpose: Recommended sequence: pressure-chain map, self-attention schematic, scaling evidence-lane chart; optional recurrence contrast and compute triangle only if final layout needs them.
caption_contract: The first three figures should teach mechanism before any company or benchmark drama appears.
blocked_claims: Generic timelines; repeated benchmark ladders; decorative diagrams; unsupported source-history claims.
next_gate: Use this sequence in the later actual SVG package.

story_purpose: Keep early visuals technical and mechanism-led, not another business-platform map, leaderboard, agent loop, or blocker grid.
caption_contract: Early figures should help the reader understand what later diagrams assume.
blocked_claims: Duplicate visual grammar; count inflation; visually pretty but technically generic figures.

story_purpose: No chart or diagram count increases until actual lightweight assets and asset-manifest rows exist.
caption_contract: This pass is a visual plan, not a completed figure package.
blocked_claims: Counting planned figures as created; using source IDs as permission for extra numbers or quotes.
next_gate: Draw assets in a later pass with manifest/provenance rows.
